antipattern.symptom: Endpoint returns 200 but with an empty / wrong-shape body. Test passes because it only checks status_code. Bug discovered in production.
antipattern.root-cause: Integration test asserts `response.status_code == 200` without checking response.data fields. Empty body = silent failure.
antipattern.fix: Always assert at least one body field. Spec endpoints[].asserts_body=true is mandatory. Use schemas in DRF + integration tests asserting the expected fields explicitly.

antipattern.symptom: Service logic test takes 3-5s per test (HTTP overhead). Coverage on service.py is high but errors only surface at the API layer.
antipattern.root-cause: Business logic is tested only through HTTP integration tests. Service methods have no direct unit tests.
antipattern.fix: Direct service tests in Test<ServiceName> class (fast, precise). Integration tests verify only the end-to-end contract (auth + serializer + service binding).

antipattern.symptom: Production incident: user A can read user B's resources. Tests pass.
antipattern.root-cause: Test suite covers anonymous-401, happy-200, validation-400 but not cross-user-403. User-owned resource lacks the ownership check assertion.
antipattern.fix: For user/org-owned resources, test_cases MUST include cross-user-403. Spec validator enforces "anonymous-401 + happy + cross-user-403 + validation/not-found".

antipattern.symptom: Tests pass on CI (SQLite), production (Postgres) crashes on deploy: case-sensitive uniqueness, JSONField ordering, partial-index behaviour differ.
antipattern.root-cause: CI runs against SQLite (default pytest-django dev DB), but production is PostgreSQL. Engine-specific behaviours diverge.
antipattern.fix: CI MUST run against PostgreSQL (the production engine). Spec ci.db_engine="postgresql" is required by the validator.
